metaTitle: Hip Pain Support & Bracing | Avana Surgical Systems
metaDescription: Hip arthritis, post-surgical hip recovery, and bursitis support. Premium bracing solutions fitted across India.

hero.title: Reclaim your stride.
hero.description: From osteoarthritis to post-surgical recovery, the right hip support helps you move with stability and less pain.
hero.imageAlt: Hip pain bracing support — landing page hero

conditions.title: Hip Osteoarthritis
conditions.description: Wear of the hip joint causing pain, stiffness, and reduced range of motion — most common in adults over 50.

conditions.title: Post-Hip Replacement
conditions.description: Recovery after total hip arthroplasty — supportive bracing and motion control protect the new joint.

conditions.title: Hip Bursitis
conditions.description: Inflammation of the bursa causing lateral hip pain — eased with offloading and rest.

conditions.title: Hip Labral Tear
conditions.description: Cartilage tear in the hip joint — often supported by activity modification and bracing.

seoContent.title: Understanding hip pain
seoContent.intro: Hip pain often shows up as groin pain, lateral pain, or stiffness — and it can affect everything from walking to sleeping.

sections.heading: Common causes
sections.body: Most chronic hip pain in adults comes from osteoarthritis, bursitis, or labral wear. Younger patients may experience pain from impingement or sports injuries.

sections.heading: When is bracing helpful?
sections.body: Hip bracing is most commonly used post-surgically — particularly after hip replacement — to protect the joint during the early recovery weeks. For arthritis, treatment is usually conservative (physiotherapy, weight management) until surgery becomes appropriate.

seoContent.disclaimer: This page is educational. Please consult an orthopaedic specialist for persistent hip pain.

faq.question: How long do I wear a hip abduction brace after hip replacement?
faq.answer: Typically 6–12 weeks, depending on your surgical approach and surgeon's protocol. Always follow your treating surgeon's instructions.

faq.question: Can bracing help hip osteoarthritis?
faq.answer: Bracing is less commonly used for hip OA than knee OA. Conservative care usually focuses on weight management, physiotherapy, and walking aids — with surgery considered when conservative options are exhausted.

cta.title: Hip pain is personal — let us help you choose right.
cta.description: Our specialists can guide you on whether bracing fits your situation, particularly after hip surgery.
